title: Docs v2.3
description: Snapshot of the released v2.3 docs line.

topics.title: Chart
topics.description: Create chart containers and choose the Lightweight Charts constructor that matches your horizontal scale.
topics.html:
The chart family is the top-level entry point for the wrapper. Start with Chart for time-based charts, then reach for OptionsChart, YieldCurveChart, or CustomChart when you need a different horizontal scale model.

Chart renders the container <div> for you and passes the chart API down through React context so nested panes, series, scales, watermarks, and primitives can attach declaratively.

Use chart variants when your data model is not the default time scale. The chart topic also owns containerProps, click handlers, crosshair handlers, and the generic ChartApiRef / GenericChartProps types exposed by the package.

See the full Basic series sample and Chart types sample for larger integrations.

api.name: Chart
api.summary: Chart component that can be used to create a chart.
examples:
```tsx
<Chart>
  <Pane stretchFactor={2}>
    ...
  </Pane>
</Chart>
```

api.name: ChartApiRef
api.summary: Chart API reference type that can be used to access the chart plugin API.

properties.summary: Internal reference to the chart API instance.

properties.summary: Function to get the chart API instance.

properties.summary: Function to clear the chart API instance.

properties.summary: Function to initialize the chart API instance.

api.summary: Chart component props.

properties.name: children
properties.summary: Children of the chart component.

properties.name: containerProps
properties.summary:
The props for the container element on which the chart will be rendered.
The wrapper sets `aria-hidden` to `true` by default because Lightweight Charts
renders layout tables internally; override it explicitly if you provide your own
accessible labelling strategy.

properties.name: onClick
properties.summary: Callback function that is called when the chart is clicked.

properties.name: onCrosshairMove
properties.summary: Callback function that is called when the crosshair moves.

properties.name: onDblClick
properties.summary: Callback function that is called when the chart is double-clicked.

properties.name: onInit
properties.summary: Callback function that is called when the chart is initialized.

properties.name: options
properties.summary: Options for the chart.

api.name: CustomChart
api.summary: CustomChart component that can be used to create a chart with a custom horizontal scale behavior.

see.label: TradingView documentation for custom horizontal scale charts

examples:
```tsx
<CustomChart horzScaleBehavior={new (defaultHorzScaleBehavior())()}>
  <LineSeries data={[{ time: "2025-01-01", value: 100 }]} />
</CustomChart>
```

topics.title: Series
topics.summary: Built-in series components, refs, and shared series props.
topics.description: Use built-in series components and shared series props without dropping down to imperative chart code.
topics.html:
Series components wrap the matching Lightweight Charts series constructors and stay close to the upstream API. The wrapper keeps the props thin: you mostly pass data, options, and a few wrapper-specific controls such as reactive, seriesOrder, and alwaysReplaceData.

When you need multiple panes, multiple series types, or price and volume overlays, you can compose them as regular React children.

For a more complete example, see the Basic series sample and Compare series sample.

topics.title: Price Lines
topics.summary: Declarative price line management for chart series.
topics.description: Attach declarative horizontal price lines to any series.
topics.html:
PriceLine is nested inside a series and manages the lifecycle of the matching upstream price line plugin for you. It is a good fit for alerts, targets, thresholds, and other reference levels that should move with the series.

The component keeps the wrapper-specific surface small: you provide the target price and pass upstream line styling through options.

See the full Price lines sample for a more interactive example.

api.name: PriceLine
api.summary: PriceLine component that can be used to add a price line to a chart pane.
examples:
```tsx
<PriceLine price={100} options={{}} />
```

api.name: PriceLineApiRef
api.summary: PriceLine API reference type that can be used to access the price line plugin API.

properties.name: api

properties.name: clear

properties.name: init
properties.summary: Function to initialize the price line API.

api.name: PriceLineProps
api.summary: PriceLine component props.

properties.name: options
properties.summary: Options for the price line.

properties.name: price
properties.summary: The price at which the price line should be drawn.

topics.title: Markers
topics.summary: Series markers and their typed props.
topics.description: Render typed marker arrays on top of a series.
topics.html:
Markers is attached to a series and exposes the upstream series markers plugin through a React component. Use it when trading signals, annotations, or point-in-time events should follow the series data rather than be rendered as custom primitives.

The wrapper-owned API is intentionally small: provide a markers array, optional marker options, and choose whether updates should be reactive.

See the full Markers sample for a richer demo.

api.name: Markers
api.summary: Markers component that can be used to add markers to a chart pane.
examples:
```tsx
<Markers
 markers={[
   { time: 1622548800, position: "aboveBar", color: "red", shape: "arrowUp" },
   { time: 1622548800, position: "belowBar", color: "green", shape: "arrowDown" },
 ]}
/>
```

api.name: MarkersApiRef
api.summary: Markers API reference type that can be used to access the series markers plugin API.

properties.name: _markers
properties.summary: Internal reference to the series markers API instance.

properties.name: api
properties.summary: Function to get the series markers API instance.

properties.name: clear
properties.summary: Function to clear the series markers API instance.

properties.name: init
properties.summary: Function to initialize the series markers API instance.

api.name: MarkersProps
api.summary: Markers component properties that can be used to create a series markers component.

properties.name: markers
properties.summary: List of markers to be displayed on the series.

properties.name: options
properties.summary: Options for the markers.

properties.name: reactive
properties.summary: Whether markers should update reactively when marker list changes.

topics.title: Watermarks
topics.summary: Text and image watermarks attached to charts.
topics.description: Add text or image watermarks to the chart using declarative components.
topics.html:
The watermark components stay close to the upstream plugin model while keeping the React composition story simple. Add a text watermark with WatermarkText or supply an image source with WatermarkImage.

Use watermarks for branding, scenario labels, or background status indicators without having to drop into imperative chart plugin code.

See the full Watermark sample for more styling ideas.

api.name: ImageWatermarkProps
api.summary: Unique properties for the image watermark component.

api.name: TextWatermarkProps
api.summary: Unique properties for the text watermark component.

api.name: WatermarkApiRef
api.summary: Watermark API reference type that can be used to access the watermark plugin API.
api.type: T extends "text" ? { _watermark: ITextWatermarkPluginApi<HorzScaleItem> | null; api: () => ITextWatermarkPluginApi<HorzScaleItem> | null; clear: () => void; init: () => ITextWatermarkPluginApi<HorzScaleItem> | null } : { _watermark: IImageWatermarkPluginApi<HorzScaleItem> | null; api: () => IImageWatermarkPluginApi<HorzScaleItem> | null; clear: () => void; init: () => IImageWatermarkPluginApi<HorzScaleItem> | null }

api.name: WatermarkImage
api.summary: Watermark component that can be used to add an image watermark to a chart pane.
examples:
```tsx
<WatermarkImage
 src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,..."
 alpha={0.5}
 padding={20}
/>
```

api.name: WatermarkText
api.summary: Watermark component that can be used to add a text or image watermark to a chart pane.
examples:
```tsx
<WatermarkText
 lines={[
   { text: "Your chart name", color: "blue", fontSize: 24 },
   { text: "Some other text", color: "pink", fontSize: 16 },
 ]}
 horzAlign="center"
/>
```

api.name: WatermarkType
api.summary:
Watermark types supported by the library.
- `text`: A text watermark.
- `image`: An image watermark.

topics.title: Time Scale
topics.summary: Time scale options, refs, and event handlers.
topics.description: Configure visible ranges, subscribe to scale events, and nest time-scale helpers under a chart.
topics.html:
TimeScale is the wrapper entry point for upstream time scale options and range-change events. Nest it inside a chart when you want to control the visible range declaratively or subscribe to time-scale updates.

The wrapper exposes thin types such as TimeScaleProps and TimeScaleApiRef, while the large option surface stays owned by Lightweight Charts itself.

See the full Scales sample for more event-driven scale usage.

api.name: TimeScale
api.summary: TimeScale component that can be used to create/customize time scale in a chart.

api.name: TimeScaleApiRef
api.summary: Time scale API reference type that can be used to access the time scale plugin API.

properties.name: init
properties.summary: Function to initialize the time scale API.

api.name: TimeScaleProps
api.summary:
Props for the TimeScale component that controls chart time scale behavior.

Use this component to configure how the chart's visible time/logical range is initialized
and updated, and to react to time scale updates via event handlers.

properties.name: children

properties.name: onSizeChange

properties.name: onVisibleLogicalRangeChange
properties.summary: Callback for when the visible logical range changes.

properties.name: onVisibleTimeRangeChange

properties.name: options

properties.name: visibleLogicalRange
properties.summary: The visible logical range for the time scale.

properties.name: visibleRange
properties.summary: The visible time range for the time scale.

topics.title: Time Scale Fit Content Trigger
topics.summary: Declarative helper for fitting the time scale to current content.
topics.description: Trigger `fitContent()` declaratively whenever a dependency list changes.
topics.html:
TimeScaleFitContentTrigger is a small wrapper-owned helper for a common chart behavior: calling fitContent() after data or layout changes. Keep it nested inside TimeScale and point deps at the values that should trigger the fit.

This helper is especially useful when data arrives asynchronously or when users toggle series and panes on and off.

signatures.summary: TimeScaleFitContentTrigger component that triggers a fit content operation on the time scale.

parameters.name: props
parameters.summary: The properties for the time scale fit content trigger.

api.name: TimeScaleFitContentTriggerProps
api.summary: TimeScaleFitContentTriggerProps component props.

properties.name: deps
properties.summary: List of dependencies that trigger the time scale to fit content.

topics.title: Price Scale
topics.summary: Declarative access to chart price scales.
topics.description: Address named price scales from React and pass through upstream options.
topics.html:
Use PriceScale inside a pane when you need to configure a specific named price scale declaratively. This is the component to reach for when you want a visible left scale, a custom scale id, or scale-specific option changes.

The wrapper keeps the API small: identify the scale with id and pass the rest of the configuration through the upstream options type.

api.name: PriceScale
api.summary: PriceScale component that can be used to create/customize price scale in a chart.
examples:
```tsx
<PriceScale id="right" options={{}} />
```

api.name: PriceScaleApiRef
api.summary: Price scale API reference type that can be used to access the price scale plugin API.

properties.summary: Function to set the ID of the price scale.

api.name: PriceScaleProps
api.summary: PriceScale component props.

properties.name: id

properties.name: options
properties.summary: Options for the price scale.

topics.title: Panes
topics.summary: Pane containers and pane refs for multi-pane charts.
topics.description: Split a chart into stacked panes and control their relative size.
topics.html:
Pane gives you declarative access to Lightweight Charts panes. Group related series into their own pane, then use stretchFactor to control how much vertical space each pane receives relative to its siblings.

Panes are also the attachment point for pane-wide primitives, so they become the natural unit for more advanced chart layouts.

See the full Panes sample for a production-style layout.

api.name: Pane
api.summary: Pane component that can be used to create a pane in a chart.
examples:
```tsx
<Pane stretchFactor={2}>
...
</Pane>
```

api.name: PaneApiRef
api.summary: Pane API reference type that can be used to access the pane plugin API.

properties.summary: Reference to the pane API.

properties.name: api
properties.summary: Function to get the pane API.

properties.name: clear
properties.summary: Function to clear the pane API.

properties.name: init
properties.summary: Function to initialize the pane API.

api.name: PaneProps
api.summary: Pane component props.

properties.name: children
properties.summary: Children of the pane component.

properties.name: stretchFactor
properties.summary: Stretch factor for the pane, which determines how much space the pane should take relative to other panes.

topics.title: Series Primitives
topics.summary: Series-scoped Lightweight Charts plugins rendered declaratively.
topics.description: Attach Lightweight Charts series plugins through a React component boundary.
topics.html:
Use SeriesPrimitive when you need custom drawing that belongs to exactly one series. The component accepts either an already-created upstream plugin instance or a render function that receives the current chart and series APIs.

For a complete visual primitive example, see the Primitives sample.

api.name: RenderPrimitive
api.summary: Render function type for series primitives.

api.name: SeriesPrimitive
api.summary: SeriesPrimitive component that can be used to create a primitive on series.
examples:
```tsx
<SeriesPrimitive render={{}} />
<SeriesPrimitive plugin={{}} />
```

api.name: SeriesPrimitiveApiRef
api.summary: Series primitive API reference type that can be used to access the series primitive plugin API.

properties.name: _primitive
properties.summary: Internal reference to the series primitive API instance.

api.name: SeriesPrimitiveProps
api.summary: Series primitive properties that can be used to create a series primitive.

topics.title: Pane Primitives
topics.summary: Pane-wide Lightweight Charts plugins rendered declaratively.
topics.description: Attach pane-wide Lightweight Charts plugins through React.
topics.html:
PanePrimitive is the pane-scoped sibling of SeriesPrimitive. Reach for it when the plugin belongs to the whole pane rather than to a specific series, such as session bands, background highlights, or pane-level overlays.

This topic is new in the v2.3 docs line and is intentionally separate from series primitives because the attachment target and the upstream plugin interfaces are different.

See the full Pane primitives sample for a richer example with pane-wide highlights.

api.name: PanePrimitive
api.summary: PanePrimitive component that can be used to create a primitive on a pane.
examples:
```tsx
<Pane>
  <PanePrimitive render={({ chart, pane }) => new MyPanePrimitive(chart, pane)} />
</Pane>
```

api.name: PanePrimitiveApiRef
api.summary: Pane primitive API reference type that can be used to access the pane primitive plugin API.

properties.name: _primitive
properties.summary: Internal reference to the pane primitive API instance.

api.name: PanePrimitiveProps
api.summary: Pane primitive properties that can be used to create a pane primitive.

api.name: RenderPanePrimitive
api.summary: Render function type for pane primitives.
